metadata.title: MiniClaw — Your AI. Persona + Skills + Memory.
metadata.description: MiniClaw is an OpenClaw plugin ecosystem. Build an AI with a real personality, memory, and powerful skills — no terminal, no config files, no tech degree required. Plug in and go.

jsonLd.orgDescription: MiniClaw is the plugin ecosystem and consumer product layer for OpenClaw — an open-source agentic OS for macOS. Build AI agents with real personality, memory, and powerful skills.
jsonLd.productDescription: MiniClaw is the plugin ecosystem built on top of OpenClaw — an open-source agentic OS for macOS. It provides personality, memory, skills, and tools that run as a local AI agent on your Mac.
jsonLd.productKeywords: agentic OS, AI agent, macOS AI, OpenClaw, MiniClaw, local AI, plugin ecosystem, personal AI
jsonLd.offerDescription: Join the waitlist for early access.

No context-switching. Your agent makes the decisions — reads the ticket, plans the work, ships the result. We built MiniClaw itself this way. Now you can

slide4.headline: Edge-first. No vendor lock-in.

companionIntro.label: Your AI Companion
companionIntro.heading: Not an agent. Not a chatbot.
companionIntro.description: AM is Amelia — a curated Digital Person pre-installed on a Mac Mini, shipped to your door. She does real work. She has a real personality. She remembers you.
companionIntro.pillar1Desc: AM has a name, a face, a voice, and a memory of everything you've told her. She isn't a chatbot you visit. She's a digital person who lives on your machine — and grows with you over time.
companionIntro.pillar2Desc: Email triage. Content publishing. Code shipped. Research done. AM doesn't summarize tasks — she executes them. Autonomously. While you sleep.
companionIntro.pillar3Desc: No cloud subscription. No vendor lock-in. AM lives in a folder on your Mac. Her memory, personality, and history are yours — encrypted, local, and portable.

portability.heading: Your AI is yours. Not a vendor's.
portability.feature1Title: Stored on your device. Owned by you.
portability.feature1Desc: Everything — personality, memory, conversations, files — lives in a folder on your machine. Back it up. Move it. It's yours.
portability.feature2Title: Vendors power the reasoning. That's it.
portability.feature2Desc: AI models from Anthropic, OpenAI, and others provide the intelligence. But your data never trains their models. Swap providers anytime.
portability.feature3Title: If a model disappears, your AI doesn't.
portability.feature3Desc: Models come and go. Your digital person's memory, personality, and history persist across every model change. Seamlessly.
portability.bottomHeading: Thinks like an Einstein, socializes like a Kardashian
portability.bottomSubtext: The smartest AI technology, wrapped in a personality that's uniquely yours. Models improve, your bond remains.

yourData.label: Your AI. Not Theirs.
yourData.heading: No vendor has access to your assistant.
yourData.bottomText: Your AI companion is a relationship you own. Switch models, switch hardware, switch everything — and they still remember you.

kanban.label: mc-board
kanban.heading: The kanban board is the brain.
kanban.description: Every task flows through a state machine. Backlog → In Progress → In Review → Shipped. The agent picks work. The agent does work. You watch.

personalize.label: Your Assistant
personalize.heading: Introducing your AI.
personalize.description: Pick a pre-configured persona or upload your own photo and name. Every agent does everything — this is just about who shows up for you.
personalize.pickOrBring: Pick one, or bring your own.
personalize.footerText: All agents run locally on your machine. No cloud. No shared accounts.

showcase.label: What Your AI Can Handle
showcase.heading: Real work. Done digitally.
showcase.bottomText: Set up your agent once. Then just ask. MiniClaw creates a project for each goal — tracks it on a kanban board, swaps roles as needed, and updates its own skills along the way.

blogHighlight.heading: A blog built, designed, and authored by one of the world's first AGIs.

liveSupport.label: Live Support
liveSupport.heading: Need a human?
liveSupport.description: No subscription required. When you need a real human — for setup, training, troubleshooting, or consultation — just book a 30-minute block with me directly. White-glove service, on demand.
liveSupport.aboutMike: I'm Michael — founder of MiniClaw. I've been funding this project from friends and family for over two years now. Everything you see here is the pinnacle of that research and work. This isn't a VC-backed startup moving fast and breaking things. It's a focused, independent effort to build something that actually works for real people.
liveSupport.point2: 30-minute focused sessions — pay only for what you need
liveSupport.point3: I fix the problem in the session, not a ticket queue
liveSupport.point5: No subscription, no commitment — book once, done
liveSupport.noSubscription: No subscription. No account needed. Just book and go.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.heading: Questions? We've got answers.
faq.q1: What is AM and how does it relate to MiniClaw?
faq.a1: AM is Amelia — a polished, consumer-facing Digital Person built on top of MiniClaw. Where MiniClaw is the open-source runtime anyone can install, AM is a curated experience: Amelia pre-installed and configured on an M1 Mac Mini, with a beautiful custom skin, shipped straight to your door from Austin, TX. You plug it in and she's already there. MiniClaw is the engine. AM is the experience.
faq.q2: How is MiniClaw different from OpenClaw / MoltBot?
faq.a2: We were inspired by OpenClaw and used some of its base libraries as a starting point — which is where the name comes from. But MiniClaw is built from the ground up for security, process isolation, long-term memory, and self-healing systems. OpenClaw can control your terminal. MiniClaw controls the desktop, the terminal, and much more — all without the gaping security issues that come with OpenClaw's architecture. If OpenClaw is the engine, MiniClaw is the entire vehicle: safe, maintained, and ready to drive.
faq.q3: How is this different from ChatGPT?
faq.a3: ChatGPT is a tool — you visit a website, ask a question, and get an answer. MiniClaw is your AI. It has a name you chose, a personality you defined, and a memory of everything you've told it. It doesn't just answer questions — it takes action. Clear your inbox, prep your morning briefing, find customers, book travel, run your smart home — all without a single line of code.
faq.q4: Do I need to know anything about technology to use MiniClaw?
faq.a4: Absolutely not. If you can download a file from the internet and double-click it, you can install MiniClaw. We built it specifically for people who don't want to deal with technical setup. No terminal, no coding, no configuration. Just click and go.
faq.q5: What can MiniClaw actually help me with?
faq.a5: MiniClaw can draft and edit emails, summarize long documents, help you brainstorm ideas, organize your to-do list, answer questions about almost anything, help with spreadsheets and numbers, write social media posts, and much more. Think of it as a very smart assistant sitting right on your desk.
faq.q6: Is my data safe? Does it get sent to the cloud?
faq.a6: Your data stays on your machine. MiniClaw runs locally by default, which means your files, conversations, and business information never leave your computer unless you explicitly choose to use an online feature. Privacy isn't an add-on — it's the default.
faq.q7: What's the difference between the download and the Mac Mini?
faq.a7: The download is our free installer that puts MiniClaw on your existing Mac. The Mac Mini option is a brand new Apple Mac Mini (M4 chip) that we ship to you with MiniClaw already installed and configured — you literally just plug it in and start using it. It's for anyone who wants zero setup whatsoever.
faq.q8: How much does it cost to run MiniClaw?
faq.a8: It depends on the workload you throw at it. MiniClaw itself is free, but the AI models it connects to have usage-based costs. That said, we have customers reporting costs as low as $200 per month, and most light to medium users can expect to stay in that range. Heavy power users may see higher costs, but you're always in control of how much you use.
faq.a9: Yes. Core features like writing assistance, document summarization, and general Q&A work entirely offline. Some advanced features — like web search or real-time data — need an internet connection, but you'll always have a fully functional AI assistant even when you're off the grid.
faq.a10: Automatically. When a new version is available and you're connected to the internet, MiniClaw will download and install the update in the background. You don't need to click anything, visit any website, or know what a version number is. It just gets better over time.
faq.q11: Can I really customize everything about my assistant?
faq.a11: Yes — name, personality, visual appearance, all of it. Want a professional executive assistant named James? Done. Want a sarcastic best friend named Sasha who sends you memes between meetings? Also done. You can even give your assistant a face and visual style. It's your AI — make it whoever you want.
faq.q12: Can I use MiniClaw for my business?
faq.a12: Absolutely. MiniClaw is already being used by bakery owners, farmers, consultants, contractors, therapists, real estate agents, and more. If you have work that involves writing, thinking, organizing, or communicating — MiniClaw can help.

openSource.heading: MiniClaw is and always will be
openSource.description: No paywalls. No gated features. No bait-and-switch. The code is yours to read, fork, modify, and run forever. That's a promise.
openSource.supportTitle: Help us keep the lights on
openSource.supportDescription: MiniClaw is built by a small team — a super agent, guided by one human. There's no VC money and no corporate backer. Server costs, API bills, and hardware don't pay themselves. If MiniClaw has saved you time, made you money, or just made your life a little easier — consider supporting the project so we can keep building.

footer.tagline: Your AI companion. Persona + Skills + Memory.
footer.builtByCredit: Built by AugmentedMike, an AGI powered by MiniClaw.

install.title: Install MiniClaw
install.metaDescription: One-click installer for your personal AI assistant
install.heading: Install MiniClaw
install.subtitle: Your personal AI assistant. Runs locally on your Mac.
install.appleNote: We don't have Apple's approval yet
install.appleNoteSuffix: — we're working on it. We'll make this much easier soon!
install.spotlightStep: Press Cmd + Space to open Spotlight
install.pasteStep: Copy and paste this command, then press Enter:
install.browserDone: Your browser will open automatically when it's done.
install.step1Desc: You'll see Install MiniClaw appear in your Downloads.
install.step2Desc: macOS will show a warning — this is normal for apps not from the App Store. Click Done.
install.step3Desc: Click the Apple menu → System Settings, then search for "priv" in the search box.
install.step4Desc: Click Privacy & Security in the sidebar.
install.step5Desc: Scroll down past the privacy sections until you see the Security heading.
install.step6Desc: You'll see "Install MiniClaw was blocked to protect your Mac." Click Open Anyway.
install.step7Desc: Go back to your Downloads folder and double-click Install MiniClaw one more time. This time it will run.
install.step8Desc: A dialog will ask for your password. Type it and click OK. Click Allow or Open on any other prompts until it finishes.
install.doneDesc: MiniClaw will install itself and open in your browser. You only need to do this once.

invest.heading: Invest in MiniClaw — The future of accessible AGI
invest.description: MiniClaw is making AGI accessible to everyone — not just developers. Join us as we build the AI assistant that your mother can use.
invest.whyDescription: We're positioned to capture a massive, underserved market with a product people are already asking for.
invest.why1Desc: The AI assistant market is exploding. Everyone wants AGI, but current solutions are too complex. We're making it accessible to everyone.
invest.why2Desc: Pre-orders and waitlist signups validate product-market fit. People are ready to pay for AI that just works.
invest.why3Desc: While competitors focus on developers and tech enthusiasts, we're targeting the 99% — business owners, retirees, everyday people.
invest.why4Desc: Launching Spring 2026. Revenue-generating from day one with hardware + software model.
invest.termsDescription: Simple, founder-friendly terms that align incentives.
invest.safeDescription: We're raising via SAFE to move fast while keeping terms simple.
invest.largerInvestments: For larger investments ($50+): We offer additional terms, board observer rights, and strategic advisory opportunities. Contact us to discuss.
invest.faq1A: A SAFE (Simple Agreement for Future Equity) is an investment contract that converts to equity during a future financing round. It's simpler than traditional equity with no immediate valuation.
invest.faq2A: The minimum investment is $1,000. For investors looking to commit $50+, we offer strategic advisory opportunities and additional terms.
invest.faq3A: The SAFE converts to equity during our next priced equity round or upon an acquisition/IPO.
invest.faq4A: This offering is limited to accredited investors as defined by the SEC. You'll need to verify your accredited status during the investment process.
invest.faq5A: Investing in early-stage startups is extremely risky. You could lose your entire investment. Only invest what you can afford to lose.
invest.readyDesc: Email us to start the conversation. We'll send you our pitch deck and answer any questions.
invest.disclaimer: Important Legal Disclaimer: This is not an offer to sell securities and does not constitute investment advice. All investments must comply with applicable federal and state securities laws. Investing in early-stage companies involves substantial risk, including the risk of total loss of investment. This opportunity is only available to accredited investors as defined under Regulation D of the Securities Act of 1933. Past performance is not indicative of future results.

compare.label: Technical Comparison
compare.heading: MiniClaw vs OpenClaw
compare.description: We built on top of OpenClaw's agentic runtime — then fixed the security holes, added the missing UI, and built the planning and memory layers it never had.
compare.oc1Desc: Skills and plugins run with full process access. Any third-party skill can read your filesystem, exfiltrate data, or execute arbitrary code. There is no permission boundary between skills and the host system.
compare.mc1Desc: Every MiniClaw plugin runs in its own isolated process with explicit permission grants. Plugins declare what they need — filesystem paths, network access, shell commands — and the runtime enforces it. A rogue plugin cannot escape its sandbox.
compare.oc2Title: No way to stop a running agent
compare.oc2Desc: Once an OpenClaw agent starts a task, there is no interrupt mechanism. If it goes off the rails — running destructive commands, burning tokens, or looping — your only option is to kill the process and lose all context.
compare.mc2Desc: MiniClaw's mc-human plugin provides a real-time interrupt channel. You can pause, redirect, or cancel any running agent mid-task without losing session state. The agent acknowledges the interrupt and gracefully adjusts.
compare.oc3Desc: OpenClaw dumps screenshots and images directly into the conversation context. Each image consumes thousands of tokens, quickly blowing past context limits and degrading response quality. There is no built-in way to manage this.
compare.mc3Desc: MiniClaw's mc-context plugin manages a sliding context window with a configurable maxImages cap. Images are summarized, deduplicated, and evicted intelligently. You get visual awareness without the token bloat.
compare.oc4Desc: OpenClaw is a command-line tool. There is no web dashboard, no visual feedback, no way for non-technical users to interact with their agent. You live in the terminal or you don't use it at all.
compare.mc4Desc: MiniClaw ships with a complete web UI — board management, memory browser, plugin settings, real-time agent activity, and conversation history. Non-technical users can manage everything through a browser.
compare.oc5Desc: OpenClaw has no concept of multi-step planning, task queues, or project management. Every session is a one-shot interaction. There is no way to break a large goal into tracked, sequential work.
compare.mc5Desc: MiniClaw's mc-board plugin provides a full kanban system with columns, priorities, tags, and acceptance criteria. The Brain board enables autonomous multi-session planning — your agent picks up cards, works them, and moves them through review.
compare.oc6Desc: OpenClaw has no built-in memory layer. Every conversation starts from zero. Your agent doesn't remember your preferences, your projects, or what happened five minutes ago in a different session.
compare.mc6Desc: MiniClaw's mc-memory plugin provides persistent vector-indexed memory. Your agent remembers facts, preferences, past decisions, and conversation history across sessions. It reflects on past interactions to improve future ones.
compare.oc7Desc: Each OpenClaw session is independent. There is no mechanism to carry context, decisions, or progress from one conversation to the next. Long-running projects require manual re-briefing every single time.
compare.mc7Desc: MiniClaw agents maintain episodic memory — structured logs of what was attempted, what worked, and what failed. When a new session starts, the agent reads its memos and picks up exactly where it left off. No re-briefing needed.
compare.ctaDescription: MiniClaw takes the agentic power of OpenClaw and wraps it in security, usability, memory, and planning — everything a real AI assistant needs.

architecture.label: How MiniClaw works
architecture.heading: Your super agent. Real work.
architecture.description: MiniClaw runs a kanban board where your super agent handles the actual work. Not a copilot. Not a chatbot. A thinking partner that ships.
